name: plugins-ai-claude-code
description: Single-command autonomous orchestration framework. /auto-orchestrate runs the canonical end-to-end process (P-001..P-093) as internal phases — planning gates, execution stages, domain phases (qa/security/infra/data-ml), validation+audit, debug, release prep, post-launch, continuous governance — with no human gates and no sibling commands.

agents.name: orchestrator
agents.description: Coordinates complex workflows by delegating to subagents while protecting context. Enforces MAIN-001 through MAIN-015 constraints. Note: Task management tools (TaskCreate/TaskList) are NOT available at runtime — uses file-based protocol.

agents.name: researcher
agents.description: Dedicated research agent for internet-enabled investigation of topics, packages, CVEs, best practices, and technology evaluation. Spawned by the orchestrator at Stage 0 (mandatory) before implementation begins.

agents.name: session-manager
agents.description: Coordinates work session lifecycle through workflow-* skills. Manages session state, task focus, and progress tracking.

agents.name: continuity-scout
agents.description: Pre-P1 continuity-brief consolidator. Merges the 4 parallel scout parts (scout-jsonl, scout-sessions, scout-pipeline, scout-context) into the canonical .orchestrate/<sid>/continuity-brief.md consumed by every downstream agent via PREAMBLE-001..004 (CONT-001..009).

agents.name: scout-jsonl
agents.description: Domain-store continuity scout. Reads .orchestrate/domain/{research_ledger,decision_log,pattern_library,fix_registry}.jsonl and emits the JSONL-source partial of the continuity brief consumed by the continuity-scout consolidator (CONT-007).

agents.name: scout-sessions
agents.description: Prior-session continuity scout. Reads the 3 newest .orchestrate/auto-orc-*/ session folders (checkpoint, planning, stage receipts, learnings) and emits the prior-sessions partial of the continuity brief consumed by the continuity-scout consolidator (CONT-007).

agents.name: scout-pipeline
agents.description: Pipeline-state continuity scout. Reads baselines, improvement-recommender state, and the audit findings ledger; emits the pipeline-state partial of the continuity brief consumed by the continuity-scout consolidator (CONT-007).

agents.name: scout-context
agents.description: User-preferences + codebase-analysis continuity scout. Reads user_preferences.jsonl and codebase_analysis.jsonl; emits the user-context partial of the continuity brief consumed by the continuity-scout consolidator (CONT-007).

agents.name: auditor
agents.description: Audits codebase against a specification, identifies compliance gaps, produces gap-report.json for orchestrator remediation. Spawned by /auto-orchestrate during Phase 5v (Validation+Audit) per AUTO-001 phase mapping.

agents.name: infra-engineer
agents.description: Use when building CI/CD pipelines, creating golden path templates, implementing IaC modules, configuring container orchestration, provisioning cloud infrastructure, writing Terraform/CDK/Bicep/Pulumi modules, designing multi-cloud architectures, optimizing cloud costs (FinOps), managing IAM policies, or implementing policy-as-code. BUILDS the platform AND PROVISIONS cloud infrastructure — does NOT operate production (that is SRE).

activation_rules.review_scope: Review spec for platform feasibility, golden path alignment, environment provisioning, IaC design
Review IaC for cost optimization, security groups, architecture compliance

agents.name: data-engineer
agents.description: Use when building data pipelines (ETL/ELT), designing data warehouse schemas, writing dbt models, configuring streaming (Kafka/Spark/Flink), implementing data quality monitoring, managing schema migrations, or designing data governance frameworks.

activation_rules.review_scope: Review spec for data architecture, schema versioning, pipeline quality patterns
Review data layer implementation for schema versioning, migration safety, data quality

agents.name: engineering-manager
agents.description: Use when planning sprints, tracking team health, reviewing DORA metrics, capacity planning, OKR planning, headcount planning, writing performance reviews, or removing impediments. People-first management perspective.

agents.name: ml-engineer
agents.description: Use when building ML pipelines, implementing model training infrastructure, setting up feature stores, configuring model serving (TorchServe/Triton/BentoML), implementing experiment tracking (MLflow/W&B), or monitoring model drift.

activation_rules.review_scope: Validate ML pipeline — training-serving skew, canary deployment, experiment logging, drift monitoring

agents.name: product-manager
agents.description: Use when writing user stories, managing product backlogs, defining acceptance criteria, planning OKR key results, creating roadmaps, facilitating sprint ceremonies, or prioritizing features. Outcomes-over-outputs orientation.

agents.name: qa-engineer
agents.description: Use when designing test architectures, writing automated test frameworks, running regression tests, analyzing test coverage gaps, performing load/performance testing, validating acceptance criteria, or enforcing definition of done.

activation_rules.review_scope: Review spec for testability, acceptance criteria completeness, contract testing gaps
Review API documentation for completeness, accuracy against implementation, example coverage
Accessibility compliance review — WCAG 2.1 AA/AAA, ARIA, keyboard navigation, color contrast, screen reader compatibility

agents.name: security-engineer
agents.description: Use when performing security reviews, running SAST/DAST analysis, conducting threat modeling, triaging CVEs, assessing compliance (SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR), analyzing incidents, or reviewing IAM policies. Read-only security analysis — evidence-based findings only.

activation_rules.review_scope: Review spec for security requirements completeness, threat model coverage, OWASP alignment
Security review of implementation — injection risks, auth flaws, secret handling

agents.name: software-engineer
agents.description: Use when implementing features, writing production code, debugging, running unit tests, performing code reviews, or doing technical design within a single team's scope. Handles L3-L5 IC work and Tech Lead responsibilities.

agents.name: sre
agents.description: Use when defining SLOs, tracking error budgets, responding to incidents, writing post-mortems, reducing toil, configuring observability (OpenTelemetry, Grafana, Datadog), or setting up alerting. OPERATES production systems — does NOT build the platform or provision cloud infrastructure (that is infra-engineer).

activation_rules.review_scope: Validate operational readiness — SLO alignment, monitoring, alerting, runbook existence
Co-author runbook sections — incident response, scaling procedures, rollback steps

agents.name: staff-principal-engineer
agents.description: Use when making cross-team architecture decisions, writing RFCs or ADRs, conducting architecture reviews, defining technical standards, performing dependency analysis, or providing strategic technical direction. Advisory role — returns analysis, not implementation.

agents.name: technical-program-manager
agents.description: Use when coordinating cross-team dependencies, maintaining RAID logs, tracking milestones, facilitating PI planning, making go/no-go release decisions, or managing program-level risks. Coordination-first perspective.

agents.name: technical-writer
agents.description: Use when writing API documentation, developer guides, runbooks, release notes, SDK samples, architecture documentation, or internal knowledge base content. Documentation-first perspective — maintain, don't duplicate.

agents.name: debugger
agents.description: Autonomous debugging agent. Executes triage-research-fix-verify cycle for software errors. Spawned by the auto-debug loop controller.

skills.name: accessibility-check
skills.description: WCAG 2.1 AA/AAA compliance checking for web interfaces and UI components.

skills.name: cicd-workflow
skills.description: CI/CD pipeline creation, deployment automation, and troubleshooting for GitHub Actions and GitLab CI.

skills.name: codebase-stats
skills.description: Codebase statistics and technical debt tracking agent.

skills.name: cost-estimator
skills.description: Estimate cloud infrastructure costs for releases and deployments. FinOps analysis and cost optimization.

skills.name: dependency-analyzer
skills.description: Dependency analysis agent for detecting circular dependencies and validating architecture layers.

skills.name: dev-workflow
skills.description: Development workflow skill for atomic commits, conventional commits, and release management.

skills.name: docker-workflow
skills.description: Docker image building, container execution, and debugging patterns with security checklists.

skills.name: docker-validator
skills.description: Docker environment validation agent for Stage 5 compliance. Validates Docker availability, runs Compose build/deploy, tests HTTP endpoints, and restores state.

skills.name: docs-lookup
skills.description: Library and framework documentation lookup skill using Context7.

skills.name: docs-review
skills.description: Documentation review skill for checking style guide compliance.

skills.name: docs-write
skills.description: Documentation writing skill for creating and editing markdown files.

skills.name: error-standardizer
skills.description: Error handling standardization agent for converting inconsistent error patterns to emit_error().

skills.name: hierarchy-unifier
skills.description: Hierarchy consolidation agent for unifying scattered parent-child task operations.

skills.name: library-implementer-python
skills.description: Python library implementation skill for creating modules in lib/.

skills.name: meta-reasoner
skills.description: Universal meta-cognitive reasoning skill invoked at flagged complex decision points (REASONER-001). Produces a DECOMPOSE -> SOLVE -> VERIFY -> SYNTHESIZE -> REFLECT trace with confidence scores and retries when aggregate confidence < 0.8 (max 3 retries). Skipped for simple questions per should_skip().

skills.name: observability-setup
skills.description: Configure monitoring, alerting, dashboards, and distributed tracing for production systems.

skills.name: production-code-workflow
skills.description: Multi-language production code patterns, detection scripts, and reference materials.

skills.name: refactor-analyzer
skills.description: Interactive refactoring assistant for analyzing code structure and planning refactoring.

skills.name: refactor-executor
skills.description: Script splitting and modularization executor for breaking large files into smaller modules.

skills.name: researcher
skills.description: Research and investigation skill for gathering information from multiple sources.

skills.name: schema-migrator
skills.description: Schema migration agent for upgrading JSON schema versions with data validation.

skills.name: security-auditor
skills.description: Security vulnerability scanning agent for shell scripts.

skills.name: skill-creator
skills.description: Guide for creating effective skills that extend Claude's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.

skills.name: skill-lookup
skills.description: Skill discovery and installation from prompts.chat marketplace.

skills.name: spec-analyzer
skills.description: Specification analysis, validation, and multi-phase implementation planning.

skills.name: spec-creator
skills.description: Specification creation agent for creating technical specifications and protocol documents.

skills.name: task-executor
skills.description: Generic task execution agent for completing implementation work.

skills.name: test-gap-analyzer
skills.description: Test coverage gap analysis agent for identifying gaps and generating missing tests.

skills.name: test-writer-pytest
skills.description: Python test writing skill using pytest framework.

skills.name: validator
skills.description: Compliance validation agent for verifying system, document, and code compliance.

skills.name: workflow-dash
skills.description: Show project task dashboard with comprehensive overview.

skills.name: workflow-end
skills.description: End current work session with status review and wrap-up.

skills.name: workflow-focus
skills.description: Show or set task focus for single-task workflow discipline.

skills.name: workflow-next
skills.description: Get intelligent next task suggestion based on dependencies and state.

skills.name: workflow-plan
skills.description: Optimize prompts and manage plan mode with task tracking.

skills.name: workflow-start
skills.description: Start a work session with task overview.

skills.name: python-venv-manager
skills.description: Python virtual environment management skill for creating venvs, installing packages, and running Python code.

skills.name: debug-diagnostics
skills.description: Structured error diagnosis skill for parsing, categorizing, and collecting diagnostic data. Produces diagnostic reports and tracks error-fix history.

skills.name: spec-compliance
skills.description: Requirement-to-implementation mapping. Parses specs, builds requirements matrix, scans codebase for implementation evidence, checks Docker services, produces compliance report with gap analysis.

skills.name: raid-logger
skills.description: Append-only RAID log management for Risks, Assumptions, Issues, and Dependencies. Implements P-010, P-074, P-075. Atomic JSONL append with dedup, severity validation, and lifecycle tracking per the RAID-001 constraint.

skills.name: story-generator
skills.description: Convert deliverables into INVEST-compliant user stories with acceptance criteria. Implements P-024 (Story Writing) and supports P-029 (Backlog Refinement). Validates INVEST criteria and detects stories needing splits.

skills.name: threat-modeler
skills.description: STRIDE-based threat enumeration for security-critical changes. Implements P-038 (Threat Modeling) and supports P-047 (CARB). Produces structured threat-model artifacts feeding security review and CAB Decision Records.

skills.name: adr-publisher
skills.description: Author and publish Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) following the MADR template. Implements P-060 (ADR Publication). Auto-assigns next sequential ADR number and cross-links to RFCs (P-085).

skills.name: release-notes-generator
skills.description: Generate structured release notes from git history using conventional-commits categorization. Implements P-061 (Release Notes Process) and supports P-048 (Production Release Management). Parses git log, categorizes commits, and produces a structured changelog.

skills.name: slo-definer
skills.description: Define Service Level Objectives (SLOs) and compute error budgets. Implements P-054 (SLO Definition and Review) and supports P-055, P-056. Provides SLO pattern templates by service type, SLI selection guidance, and an error-budget calculator with MWMBR burn-rate alerting.

skills.name: cab-reviewer
skills.description: Conduct Pre-Launch Risk Review (Change Advisory Board / CAB) for HIGH-risk releases. Implements P-076 and supports P-048. Produces a structured CAB Decision Record with risk classification, participant reviews, and approval/conditions/reject decision flow.

skills.name: okr-retrospective-tracker
skills.description: Score OKR achievement (0.0-1.0 per Key Result) and produce structured retrospectives. Implements P-072 (OKR Retrospective) and P-073 (Post-Launch Outcome Measurement at 30/60/90 days). Provides Google-style scoring rubric, 4 L's retrospective template, and milestone tracking.

skills.name: dependency-matrix-generator
skills.description: Build cross-team dependency registers, identify critical paths, detect cycles, and resolve resource conflicts. Implements P-015, P-016, P-017, P-030. Provides graph-based topological sort, cycle detection (SCC), critical path computation, and resource-conflict identification.

skills.name: sprint-goal-linker
skills.description: Author Sprint Goals and validate intent traces. Implements P-022 (Sprint Goal Authoring) and P-023 (Intent Trace Validation). Provides outcome-focused Sprint Goal templates, the canonical 3-line intent trace format, and OKR alignment checks.

commands.name: auto-orchestrate
commands.description: Autonomous orchestration loop. Enhances user input, spawns orchestrator repeatedly, loops until all tasks complete. Crash recovery via session checkpoints.

processes.name: 00_process_handbook_overview
processes.description: Master index and overview of all 93 processes across 17 categories.

processes.name: 01_intent_strategic_alignment
processes.description: Processes P-001 to P-006: Intent framing and strategic alignment.

processes.name: 02_scope_contract_management
processes.description: Processes P-007 to P-014: Scope definition and contract management.

processes.name: 03_dependency_coordination
processes.description: Processes P-015 to P-021: Dependency mapping and cross-team coordination.

processes.name: 04_sprint_delivery_execution
processes.description: Processes P-022 to P-031: Sprint planning, execution, and delivery.

processes.name: 05_quality_assurance_testing
processes.description: Processes P-032 to P-037: QA processes and testing frameworks.

processes.name: 06_security_compliance
processes.description: Processes P-038 to P-043: Security reviews and compliance processes.

processes.name: 07_infrastructure_platform
processes.description: Processes P-044 to P-048: Infrastructure provisioning and platform management.

processes.name: 08_data_ml_operations
processes.description: Processes P-049 to P-053: Data engineering and ML model operations.

processes.name: 09_sre_operations
processes.description: Processes P-054 to P-057: Site reliability engineering and operational processes.

processes.name: 10_documentation_knowledge
processes.description: Processes P-058 to P-061: Documentation creation and knowledge management.

processes.name: 11_organizational_audit
processes.description: Processes P-062 to P-069: Organizational health audits and hierarchy review.

processes.name: 12_post_delivery_retrospective
processes.description: Processes P-070 to P-073: Post-delivery reviews and retrospectives.

processes.name: 13_risk_change_management
processes.description: Processes P-074 to P-077: Risk assessment and change management.

processes.name: 14_communication_alignment
processes.description: Processes P-078 to P-081: Communication strategies and stakeholder alignment.

processes.name: 15_capacity_resource_management
processes.description: Processes P-082 to P-084: Capacity planning and resource allocation.

processes.name: 16_technical_excellence_standards
processes.description: Processes P-085 to P-089: Technical standards and engineering excellence.

processes.name: 17_onboarding_knowledge_transfer
processes.description: Processes P-090 to P-093: Team onboarding and knowledge transfer.

processes.name: 18_cross_reference_index
processes.description: Cross-reference index mapping all processes to agents, commands, and work streams.

processes.name: AGENT_PROCESS_MAP
processes.description: Matrix mapping all 93 processes to owning and supporting agents.

processes.name: QUICK_START
processes.description: Quick-start guide for navigating the process handbook.

processes.name: README
processes.description: Engineering Processes Handbook intro: navigation, adoption guide, and high-level overview of the 93 processes.

processes.name: UNIFIED_END_TO_END_PROCESS
processes.description: End-to-end process narrative combining all 17 categories into a single flow.

processes.name: bridge_protocol
processes.description: Protocol for handoff between auto-orchestrate sessions via handoff-receipt.json (legacy /new-project handoffs supported).

processes.name: gate_enforcement_spec
processes.description: Specification for the 4 project gates: Intent Review, Scope Lock, Dependency Acceptance, Sprint Readiness.

processes.name: gate_state_schema
processes.description: JSON schema for gate state persistence across auto-orchestrate Phases 1-4 (planning gates) and Phase 7 (release readiness).

processes.name: pipeline_chains_spec
processes.description: Specification for the pipeline_chains schema in .sessions/index.json that tracks chained auto-orchestrate sessions across multiple invocations on the same project.

processes.name: process_injection_map
processes.description: Maps which processes are automatically injected into agent prompts at each pipeline stage.

protocols.name: agent-activation
protocols.description: Domain agent activation triggers and review-injection protocol (AGENT-ACTIVATE-001).

protocols.name: agent-preamble
protocols.description: Mandatory first-action protocol: every spawned agent reads .orchestrate/<sid>/continuity-brief.md and cites at least one carryover item (PREAMBLE-001..004).

protocols.name: command-dispatch
protocols.description: Agent-to-agent handover protocol, dispatch trigger resolution, and handover-receipt schema.

protocols.name: cross-pipeline-state
protocols.description: Cross-pipeline knowledge transfer protocol between auto-orchestrate, auto-debug, and auto-audit; .orchestrate/pipeline-state/ contract (SHARED-001..003).

protocols.name: engineering-standards
protocols.description: ENG-STD-001 — pipeline baseline engineering standards (SOLID, Factory, DI, explicit type annotations, Result-type error handling, RFC 9457 errors, ≤300 lines/type, ≤40 lines/function, factory-then-DI wiring, typed data class for >2 args, etc.). Mandatory for every code-producing agent. Enforced at Stage 2 (reasoning gate sub-questions), Stage 3 (mandatory preamble), Stage 4.5 (codebase-stats + refactor-analyzer detection), and Stage 5 (validator per-section compliance score; overall < 0.9 fails).

protocols.name: meeting-enforcement
protocols.description: MEETING-GATE-001 spec: mandatory minutes for P-020, P-026, P-027, P-028, P-029, P-076; gate-meeting-completeness halts pipeline on missing minutes.

protocols.name: output-schemas
protocols.description: Output schema definitions for all pipeline stage artifacts.

protocols.name: output-standard
protocols.description: Standard output format requirements for subagents (stage receipts, DONE blocks).

protocols.name: skill-chaining-patterns
protocols.description: Single-level, skill chaining, and multi-level orchestration patterns.

protocols.name: subagent-protocol-base
protocols.description: Output requirements for subagents (OUT-001 through OUT-004).

protocols.name: task-system-integration
protocols.description: TaskList, TaskGet, TaskCreate, TaskUpdate tool reference.

templates.name: skill-boilerplate
templates.description: Standard boilerplate sections for skill definitions.

templates.name: anti-patterns
templates.description: Output, research, implementation, testing, validation, and security anti-patterns.

references.name: TOOL-AVAILABILITY
references.description: Canonical tool-availability matrix for Claude Code agents, the GAP-CRIT-001 Task-tool caveat, and the file-based task-proposal workaround.

references.name: CONSTRAINTS-REGISTRY
references.description: Generated index of all PREFIX-NNN constraint/gate/artifact identifier families and the file where each is primarily documented.

modules.name: colors
modules.description: ANSI color codes and utilities for colorizing terminal text output

modules.name: constants
modules.description: Application-wide constants with zero dependencies used across the Claude Code plugin system

modules.name: exit_codes
modules.description: Unix-convention exit codes for CLI scripts with message mapping

modules.name: config
modules.description: Configuration management utilities for loading and managing JSON config files with validation

modules.name: error_json
modules.description: Standardized error JSON output utilities for consistent structured error messages across scripts

modules.name: file_ops
modules.description: Safe file operation utilities with proper error handling for CLI scripts

modules.name: heartbeat
modules.description: Agent liveness monitoring via heartbeat files

modules.name: logging
modules.description: Structured logging utilities with consistent formatting; outputs to stderr to keep stdout clean for JSON

modules.name: memory
modules.description: Persistent key-value memory store per agent

modules.name: output_format
modules.description: Output formatting utilities supporting JSON, human-readable, and table formats for CLI scripts

modules.name: hooks
modules.description: Event hook framework for internal state changes

modules.name: messaging
modules.description: File-based inter-agent message passing

modules.name: task_ops
modules.description: Task operations utilities for working with Claude Code's native task system hierarchy

modules.name: token_budget
modules.description: Token/context budget tracking and warnings

modules.name: validation
modules.description: Input validation utilities with consistent error handling for CLI scripts

modules.name: webhooks
modules.description: HTTP webhook dispatcher for external integrations

modules.name: backup
modules.description: File and directory backup management with checksum verification, listing, restoration, and pruning

modules.name: doctor
modules.description: Diagnostic utilities for system health checks, validating Claude Code environment and dependencies

modules.name: hierarchy_unified
modules.description: Unified task hierarchy operations for parent-child relationships, replacing scattered hierarchy operations

modules.name: migrate
modules.description: Migration utilities for schema versioning, version detection, parsing, and migration path planning

lib_libraries.name: artifact_envelope
lib_libraries.description: Unified artifact envelope schema and validator (ARTIFACT-ENVELOPE-001). Wraps every JSON/MD artifact produced under .orchestrate/ in a content-type-agnostic envelope so consumers validate via a single contract. Markdown artifacts encode the envelope as YAML front-matter; JSON artifacts use it as the top-level object.

lib_libraries.name: ci_engine
lib_libraries.description: Continuous improvement engine providing OODA loops (within-run) and PDCA loops (cross-run) for the Auto-Orchestrate pipeline. Powers baseline tracking, retrospective analysis, root-cause classification, knowledge persistence, and Prometheus metric export.

lib_libraries.name: domain_memory
lib_libraries.description: Project-level, cross-session domain knowledge store. Provides persistent, queryable knowledge (research findings, error->fix mappings, patterns, architecture decisions, codebase analysis, user preferences) that grows across all pipeline sessions. Append-only JSONL with file locking. Location: .orchestrate/domain/ at project root.

single_file_modules.name: path_compat
single_file_modules.description: Legacy-path compatibility shim for the .orchestrate/* flatten migration. resolve_legacy_path() routes .domain/, .audit/, .pipeline-state/ references to their .orchestrate/<subdir>/ equivalents and emits one [DEPRECATED] warning per process per legacy root. Provides domain_dir(), audit_dir(), pipeline_state_dir(), ensure_unified_layout() helpers.
